find: - **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni correction applied only to secondary endpoints (≤2 comparisons) with α = 0.025 for each secondary test. **Harmonisation note:** This threshold supersedes any conflicting values in THEORY.md; the single adopted value is α=0.05 (two-sided) for primary, with Bonferroni-adjusted α=0.025 for secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.

find: - **Justification for σ = 10:** σ = 10 is a conservative estimate based on published PAM-13 SD range (9–11) from Hibbard et al. (2004, PMID 15333167; Health Serv Res 39(4 Pt 1):1005–26). This will be updated after pilot phase (N=20 per group) if observed SD deviates >20% from assumed value.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk description | Probability (1–5) | Impact (1–5) | Mitigation strategy |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Low patient recruitment (target N=132 not met) | 3 | 4 | Multi-site recruitment; extended enrollment period by 3 months; contingency budget for advertising |
| 2 | High dropout rate (>20%) | 3 | 3 | Regular follow-up calls; financial incentive for completion; per-protocol sensitivity analysis |
| 3 | Technical failure of LLM platform (downtime >48h) | 2 | 4 | Redundant server infrastructure; weekly backups; rollback plan to standard care |
| 4 | Data privacy breach (PII exposure) | 1 | 5 | End-to-end encryption; access logs; DSMB oversight; GDPR-compliant data handling |
| 5 | Contamination of control group (patients access similar AI tools) | 2 | 3 | Monitoring of control group via self-report; exclusion of patients using external AI health tools |
| 6 | Effect size smaller than assumed (δ < 5.4) | 3 | 4 | Interim analysis at N=100 for futility; conditional power threshold of 20% for stopping |
| 7 | Adverse events in intervention arm >5% | 2 | 5 | Monthly DSMB review; stopping rule triggers immediate halt; safety monitoring plan in place |

**Note:** This is the single consolidated risk matrix. All previous versions in core documents are superseded.

heading: ## Limitations
body:
1. **Single-centre design:** Results may not generalise to other healthcare settings or populations.
2. **Self-report primary outcome:** PAM-13 is a validated but subjective measure; social desirability bias may inflate scores.
3. **Digital literacy confound:** Patients with low digital literacy may be underrepresented or have differential engagement.
4. **Hawthorne effect:** Both arms may show improvement due to trial participation alone.
5. **Placebo effect in intervention arm:** Interaction with an AI assistant may produce non-specific benefits.
6. **Assumed standard deviation (σ=10):** If true SD deviates >20%, sample size may be inadequate; pilot phase will update this.
7. **Reference integrity limitations:** Some foundational references (e.g., Hibbard et al. 2009 proprietary manual) are not publicly verifiable; key framework references (Tao et al. 2026, Blumenthal-Lee 2024, Tkemaladze 2026) are pre-prints with TBD DOIs and thus not yet citable as published evidence.
8. **Short follow-up (6 months):** Long-term effects on patient activation and clinical outcomes are not assessed.
9. **Single primary endpoint:** May miss broader impacts on health behaviours or quality of life.

**Note:** This is the single consolidated limitations list. All previous versions in core documents are superseded.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead PI:** [Name TBD] — overall coordination, regulatory compliance
- **Co-I (Clinical):** [Name TBD] — patient recruitment, clinical oversight
- **Co-I (Technical):** [Name TBD] — LLM platform development, data infrastructure
- **Potential partners (in negotiation):**
  - Insignia Health (PAM-13 licensing, MCID validation)
  - Fraunhofer IGD (AI explainability, visualisation)
  - Tbilisi State University (TSU) — patient recruitment site
  - University of Copenhagen (UCPH) — statistical analysis, external validation
- **Status:** All partner agreements are preliminary; formal contracts pending funding confirmation.

**Note:** This is the single consolidated partner list. All previous versions in core documents are superseded.
